breaking_news.title: GlassWorm malware hits 400+ code repos on GitHub, npm, VSCode
breaking_news.summary:
The GlassWorm supply-chain campaign has returned with a coordinated attack targeting hundreds of packages and repositories on GitHub, npm, and VSCode extensions. Threat actors are leveraging stolen tokens to force-push malware into popular Python projects and development tools.

This widespread compromise affects developers globally, potentially injecting malicious code into trusted software dependencies. The incident highlights significant risks in the open-source ecosystem and necessitates immediate auditing of development environments.

breaking_news.title: Stryker attack wiped tens of thousands of devices, no malware needed
breaking_news.summary:
Medical technology giant Stryker confirmed a cyberattack that remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices through its internal Microsoft environment. The pro-Palestinian hacktivist group Handala claimed responsibility, stating they exfiltrated approximately 50TB of corporate data.

While medical devices remain safe, electronic ordering systems are offline, forcing manual operations across 79 countries. This disruption underscores the vulnerability of critical healthcare infrastructure to hacktivist groups targeting enterprise networks.

breaking_news.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers disclosed a critical unpatched vulnerability in the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon allowing unauthenticated remote code execution with root privileges. Tracked as CVE-2026-32746, the flaw carries a CVSS score of 9.8 due to an out-of-bounds write in the LINEMODE Set.

This high-severity flaw impacts legacy systems and network infrastructure relying on telnet services without immediate mitigation. Organizations must urgently disable telnet or apply patches to prevent potential system compromise by remote attackers.

breaking_news.title: AI Flaws in Amazon Bedrock, LangSmith, and SGLang Enable Data Exfiltration
breaking_news.summary:
Security researchers identified a flaw in AWS Bedrock AgentCore Code Interpreter that permits data exfiltration and remote command execution via DNS queries. The sandbox mode, designed for isolation, allows outbound DNS queries that attackers can exploit to establish covert communication channels.

This vulnerability compromises the security promises of agentic AI workflows on a major cloud platform, potentially exposing sensitive enterprise data. AWS has acknowledged the report, but the finding highlights architectural risks in AI execution environments.

breaking_news.title: UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms
breaking_news.summary:
The UK government agency Companies House confirmed a security flaw in its WebFiling service exposed details of millions of firms since October 2025. The vulnerability allowed users to view confidential director details and potentially alter company records before the service was taken offline.

This breach impacts the integrity of the UK corporate registry and exposes sensitive business information to unauthorized access. The incident forces a review of government digital infrastructure security and data protection protocols for public registries.

breaking_news.title: Nine critical vulnerabilities in Linux AppArmor put over 12M enterprise systems at risk
breaking_news.summary:
Qualys researchers disclosed nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux AppArmor security module, collectively dubbed CrackArmor. These flaws allow unprivileged local attackers to gain root access, break container isolation, and crash systems without administrative credentials.

Over 12 million enterprise Linux instances running Ubuntu, Debian, and SUSE are potentially at risk due to the module's default deployment. The discovery necessitates urgent patching across cloud platforms, IoT devices, and edge environments to prevent privilege escalation.

breaking_news.title: Crypto e-commerce platform Bitrefill accuses North Korea of stealing 18,500 purchase records
breaking_news.summary:
Crypto e-commerce platform Bitrefill accused North Korea's Lazarus group of stealing around 18,500 purchase records containing email and crypto payment addresses. The attackers accessed metadata including IP addresses, indicating a targeted espionage or financial theft operation.

This incident highlights the ongoing threat state-sponsored actors pose to cryptocurrency infrastructure and user privacy. Affected users face potential phishing risks, while the breach reinforces the need for enhanced security in digital asset platforms.

breaking_news.title: Medusa ransomware gang claims attacks on prominent Mississippi hospital, New Jersey county
breaking_news.summary:
The Medusa ransomware gang claimed a devastating cyberattack on the largest hospital in Mississippi, knocking out systems for nine days. The operation disrupted critical healthcare services and patient care during the outage period.

This attack demonstrates the continued targeting of healthcare infrastructure by ransomware groups seeking operational disruption. Hospitals must prioritize resilience planning to mitigate the impact of future ransomware incidents on patient safety.

breaking_news.title: Storm-2561 Uses Fake Fortinet, Ivanti VPN Sites to Drop Hyrax Infostealer
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actor Storm-2561 is using fake Fortinet and Ivanti VPN sites to distribute the Hyrax infostealer through SEO poisoning campaigns. Victims searching for legitimate VPN clients are tricked into downloading malware that steals corporate login credentials.

This campaign exploits user trust in search results and brand names to compromise enterprise security perimeters. Organizations need to educate employees on verifying software sources and implementing strict access controls to prevent credential theft.

breaking_news.title: Russia-linked APT uses DRILLAPP backdoor to spy on Ukrainian targets
breaking_news.summary:
Russia-linked APT group Laundry Bear is targeting Ukrainian entities with a new DRILLAPP backdoor campaign using Microsoft Edge debugging for stealth. The operation shares tactics with previous espionage efforts against Ukrainian defense forces and uses charity-themed lures.

This activity indicates persistent state-sponsored espionage efforts in the region, focusing on intelligence collection rather than bulk data theft. Defenders must monitor for suspicious PowerShell activity and Edge debugging artifacts to detect these intrusions.
